// Principles

Styling rules

The principles I'm using in every fit.

Casual shirts → untucked, always

  • Every button-up casual shirt is worn untucked with sleeves rolled to forearm.
  • Exception: the white formal shirt is tucked for smart-formal occasions only.

The four vibes

  • Casual — relaxed everyday, no-effort: tees + chinos/joggers + white sneakers.
  • Everyday — smart-casual, your default for class/coffee/dinner with parents.
  • Street — oversized tees + cargos/joggers + statement shoes (AF1s shine here).
  • Resort — linen, summery, vacation, brunch, breezy fabrics.

Sneaker logic

  • Custom AF1s: oversized tees + black cargo or khaki joggers. They're a statement; pants stay quiet.
  • Court Vision (orange swoosh): your "white sneaker" workhorse. Orange ties into warm graphics, mustard/khaki, salmon. Works everywhere.
  • Olive Crocs: earth-tone streetwear only. Joggers + plain tees, comfort fits.
  • Navy loafers: MVP. Pairs with ~80% of the wardrobe — shirts, polos, linen, chinos.

Sunglasses logic new

  • Round black + orange-tint lenses: warm, soft, retro. Pairs with resort fits, earth tones, polos, anything with sage/cream/brown/salmon. The amber tint speaks to warm palettes.
  • Square black Hawkers: sharp, modern, slightly aggressive. Best with cobalt streetwear, all-black evening fits, AF1s, and the new pleated pants. When you need attitude.

Sock logic new

  • With loafers: always invisible — grey no-show, black/grey-stripe no-show, or low-cut white/sage. The shoe leather is the statement; socks shouldn't peek.
  • With Court Visions (white sneakers): mix of subtle and statement. Sporty crew socks (Skechers navy, Skechers yellow) for active fits; ankle socks for resort.
  • With AF1s: statement socks earn their place here. The shoes are loud; loud socks complement rather than fight. Watermelons, cheeses, Scooby — all valid.
  • With Crocs: no socks, or technical socks only. Don't ruin the lazy energy.
  • Statement socks rule: only one statement item per outfit. If the shoes are loud (AF1s), the socks can match the energy. If the shirt is loud (floral, toucan), socks stay invisible.

Black pleated pants new

  • The dressiest pants you own now. Pleats add structure that flat trousers can't match — they sit cleaner, drape better, and read as intentional.
  • Pairs with: any shirt (untucked or tucked), oversized tees (creates a high-low tension that reads as fashion-aware), beige knit polo (peak fit).
  • Shoes: navy loafers default. AF1s for the wildcard streetwear move.
  • Sunglasses: square Hawkers — pleats demand sharp accessories.
  • Socks: black no-show with grey stripe (Adidas) — invisible but technical.

Color combos that ALWAYS work

  • White + navy — universal, always sharp
  • Brown/tan + sage — Italian summer's signature
  • Black + grey + navy — sharp evening base
  • Pink + sage — complementary, unexpected, soft
  • Cobalt + black — confident streetwear
  • Orange + navy — classic nautical/preppy

Avoid these

  • Cobalt + teal — close but not matching
  • White linen + olive Crocs — vibe mismatch
  • AF1s + soft preppy fits — overpowers
  • Pink + red — clashing warm tones
  • Statement socks + statement shirt — too many loud notes
  • Sage + olive — two close greens fight
